findings.evidence: Current lines 41 and 47 show `variable ... {ι : Type*}` with `noncomputable def signPattern`; the diff shows this used to be under `variable ... {ι : Type*} [Finite ι]`, and there was no `omit [Finite ι] in` on the definition itself.
findings.fix: Preserve the old public API, or move the generalized definition to a new name and leave a dated `@[deprecated]` compatibility alias for the old finite-signature entry point.
findings.issue: `signPattern` is public and no longer carries the previous `[Finite ι]` instance argument after the file-level variable was changed, so explicit downstream uses of the old constant arity can break despite the PR claiming no public signatures changed.

findings.evidence: Current line 168 defines `noncomputable def signHom` under the non-finite variable block at line 41; the diff only had `omit [Finite ι] in` for `signHom_apply` and `signHom_injective`, not for `signHom` itself, so the old public definition inherited `[Finite ι]`.
findings.fix: Keep `signHom` at the old finite-signature API or introduce the generalized hom under a new name and provide a dated deprecated wrapper for the old signature.
findings.issue: `signHom` is public and likewise loses the inherited `[Finite ι]` argument, changing the exported declaration signature without a deprecation path.

summary: The cleanup removes an unused file-level instance, but that silently changes the public signatures of `signPattern` and `signHom`. Deleted helpers are private and the finite Galois-group results keep their `[Finite ι]` requirement.
